_comment: Change-detection thresholds. A diff smaller than these is considered noise and won't trigger any downstream republish. Larger diffs write a change entry to data/state/changes.json.
_scope_note: Commodities backed by OilPriceAPI + live-data.js (WTI, Brent, Natural Gas, Gasoline RBOB, Heating Oil, Diesel ULSD, Jet Fuel, Newcastle Coal, Dutch TTF, JKM LNG, Gold, etc.) are EXCLUDED from change detection. live-data.js refreshes them client-side every 5 minutes, so server-side rendering would just create noise in git history. Thresholds below only apply to hardcoded values that do NOT have a live-data.js feed.

_default._note: Applies to any future hardcoded commodity

gas_prices_national._note: Gas prices are fully server-rendered — no client-side live data feed. 0.3 cent move = ~$0.60/week driver impact — meaningful.

gas_prices_by_state._note: Individual state change threshold. Watch for CA, OK, MS, TX (top/bottom).

_rationale: These thresholds match market-practitioner intuition about what's newsworthy, not the finest tick the API reports.
